title: Incident Response Training Completion Tracking

compliance.assurance_rationale: NIST 800-53 IR-2 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIST 800-53 IR-2 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.14.44: Incident Response Training Completion Tracking.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.14.44' running on lms:completion HEC JSON or snow:training_task if modeled, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: HTTP Event Collector (HEC), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928)
spl:
index=hr_training sourcetype=lms:completion earliest=-365d course_id="IR-101"
| stats latest(completed_at) as completed by user_id
| inputlookup append=t ir_required_roles.csv
| stats first(completed) as completed, values(role) as roles by user_id
| where isnull(completed) AND roles="SOC"
description: Joins LMS completion events with HR role for IR-2 incident response training evidence.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIST 800-53 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes hr_training and sourcetypes lms:completion. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Hash user identifiers consistently; (2) refresh role lookup from IdM; (3) quarterly compliance report; (4) store exports for assessors. Indexes required: `index=hr_training`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=lms:completion`.
visualization: Table (missing training), Single value (% complete), Bar chart (by team). Key fields: `completed`, `roles`, `user_id`.
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
grandmaExplanation: We watch for joins lms completion events with hr role for incident response training evidence. This helps maintain steady compliance posture and reduces manual review effort.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: HTTP Event Collector (HEC), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `lms:completion` HEC JSON or `snow:training_task` if modeled.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Hash user identifiers consistently; (2) refresh role lookup from IdM; (3) quarterly compliance report; (4) store exports for assessors.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=hr_training sourcetype=lms:completion earliest=-365d course_id="IR-101"
| stats latest(completed_at) as completed by user_id
| inputlookup append=t ir_required_roles.csv
| stats first(completed) as completed, values(role) as roles by user_id
| where isnull(completed) AND roles="SOC"
```

Understanding this SPL

**Incident Response Training Completion Tracking (IR-2)** — Joins LMS completion events with HR role for IR-2 incident response training evidence.

Documented **Data sources**: `lms:completion` HEC JSON or `snow:training_task` if modeled. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): HTTP Event Collector (HEC), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: hr_training; **sourcetype**: lms:completion. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=hr_training, sourcetype=lms:completion, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by user_id** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• Loads rows via `inputlookup` (KV store or CSV lookup) for enrichment or reporting.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by user_id** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• Filters the current rows with `where isnull(completed) AND roles="SOC"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (missing training), Single value (% complete), Bar chart (by team)
